extracted.chunk_summary: This text discusses various deep learning network architectures for real-time semantic segmentation tasks, including single-branch, dual-branch, and U-shaped networks. The performance of these networks is evaluated on various datasets, including Cityscapes, CamVid, ADE20K, UAVid, and LoveDA. The results show that U-shaped networks achieve better accuracy and real-time performance compared to single-branch and dual-branch networks.

extracted.chunk_summary: This article presents a method for real-time semantic segmentation of drones that integrates multi-scale global context information. The method achieves high accuracy and real-time performance on the UAvid and UDD6 datasets.

claims.text: Semantic segmentation is a novel and challenging task in various image processing tasks for drone remote sensing.

evidence.quote: Semantic segmentation is a novel and challenging task in various image processing tasks for drone remote sensing.

claims.text: Semantic segmentation aims to classify every pixel in an image based on its semantic information.

evidence.quote: Pre-trained models are usually trained on massive amounts of data, which improves the efficiency and stability of training.

evidence.quote: Adopting a large pre-trained model not only improves accuracy but also brings high computational complexity.

extracted.chunk_summary: This chunk discusses the limitations of CNN convolutions in understanding images and proposes a new model that uses a UNet structure with a ResNet18 encoder and a global local attention mechanism to extract semantic information about features.

claims.text: The proposed model has a global–local attention module.

evidence.quote: In the global–local attention module, both a global branch and a local branch were designed to improve the model’s ability to extract and fuse contextual information from feature maps.

claims.text: The global branch applies pooling operations along the x-axis and y-axis.

evidence.quote: In the global branch, pooling operations are applied along the x-axis and y-axis, compressing the information in these directions and embedding it into the feature map.

claims.text: The local branch applies convolution operations with kernel sizes of 1 × 1 and 3 × 3.

evidence.quote: Therefore, an additional local branch is designed, utilizing convolution operations with kernel sizes of 1 × 1 and 3 × 3 to capture the relevant information between neighboring pixels.

claims.text: The global and local branches are fused to enable interaction between global and local information.

evidence.quote: The global and local branches are then fused, enabling interaction between global and local information, which enhances the algorithm’s accuracy.

claims.text: The global attention branch utilizes coordinate attention to extract global information.

evidence.quote: The global attention branch utilizes coordinate attention to extract global information, considering both inter-channel relationships and positional information.

claims.text: The local branch applies two convolutions with kernel sizes of 3 and 1.

evidence.quote: The local branch applies two convolutions with kernel sizes of 3 and 1 to capture local information from the feature map.

The model's performance is tested on the UAVid and UDD6 datasets.

extracted.chunk_summary: The paper proposes a real-time semantic segmentation algorithm based on a channel attention mechanism, comprising an encoder, a global–local attention module, and a shallow-feature fusion module. The model achieves a real-time performance of 72.4 FPS with an input resolution of 1024 ×1024 on the UAVid test set, with an accuracy of 68% mIoU. The architecture is designed to enhance features by integrating characteristics from different stages of the encoder.

claims.text: The shallow-feature fusion module utilizes features from the U-Net’s down-sampling layers.
